items.title: Millennium and Point72 gain as hedge funds rebound from Iran war
items.summary: Izzy Englander and Steve Cohen’s firms post double-digit returns in first half of year

items.title: New Zealand House Prices Near Three-Year Low on Iran War Worries
items.summary: New Zealand house prices fell for the third straight month in June, nearing a three-year low and adding to signs of an economic slowdown as the global oil shock hits household incomes and consumer confidence.

items.title: ECB Views Splinter on Next Rate Move as Inflation Sinks With Oil
items.summary: Just three weeks after the European Central Bank unanimously raised interest rates in response to the Iran war, there’s no consensus on what to do next.

items.title: US Crude Grades Shed Iran War Premium as Energy Crisis Eases
items.summary: Key US oil grades have slumped to trade at a discount again as a gush of crude flows through the Strait of Hormuz and a recent spike in demand for American exports fades.

items.title: Wall Street Worries Less Fed Talk Will Spur Market Volatility
items.summary: It’s only been a few weeks since Kevin Warsh took the helm of the Federal Reserve, but analysts are already concerned that one of his key promises — a quieter central bank — will lead to more volatility in the market.

items.title: Greek politician's mother dies of wounds after arson attack
items.summary: Three separate attacks involving petrol bombs and other improvised devices took place in Thessaloniki in the early hours of Wednesday.

items.title: Ukraine Takes the War to Russia
items.summary: Kyiv’s aim is simple: Put enough pressure on President Vladimir Putin that he agrees to end the war. Will it work?

items.title: With a Fragile Ceasefire under Threat, What Future for the Strait of Hormuz?
items.summary: The U.S.-Israeli war with Iran prompted Tehran to close the Strait of Hormuz, allowing only selected ships to pass through. In this Q&A, Crisis Group experts Yasmine Farouk, Chris Newton and Ali Vaez consider what could come next in this vital maritime trade corridor.

items.title: Ship runs aground in Strait of Hormuz, Iran state TV reports
items.summary:
A ship ran aground in the Strait of Hormuz while using a route not approved by Iran, state television in Tehran reported on Wednesday. The vessel was identified as a foreign container ship, with no other details.
The report appeared aimed at underlining Tehran’s claims to control the strait, which the world has long considered an international waterway. A fifth of all oil and natural gas passed th

items.title: REalloys Announces Partnership to Operate on US Army Base
items.summary:
REalloys CEO Lipi Sternheim said that members of the US military could be actively involved in working with his company to mine rare earth minerals after the announcement of a deal to let REalloy mine on US Army bases. 
Sternheim said that the partnership with the Army shows how 'strategically important' rare earth metals are to the United States.  (Source: Bloomberg)

items.title: Бывший разведчик объяснил покушение на Ермолаева в Монако
A Long Gulf War Can Starve the World. A Hormuz Transit Deal Could Save Millions.
EU and NATO Members Grapple with War in Iran
items.summary: This week on War & Peace, Olga speaks with Crisis Group’s head of EU Affairs, Lisa Musiol, about how the EU, its members and European members of NATO have responded to the U.S.-Israeli war with Iran and what it reveals about EU foreign policy and its constraints.

items.title: China’s pressure campaign tests Trump’s commitment to Japan
items.summary:
Beijing’s move this week to add 20 more Japanese entities to its export-control list is the latest chapter in a complex US-Japan-China geopolitical triangle.
Over the past seven months, an angry China has punished Tokyo repeatedly. A miffed Japan is left feeling betrayed. And a war-distracted Washington has struggled to navigate between the two, adding further tension in a region already on tenter

items.title: Israel kills three Palestinians in Gaza despite ‘ceasefire’

items.title: U.S. and Iran Set to Hold Indirect Talks After Trading Attacks: What to Know
